mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically positive at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, creating a compounding demand shock that pushes equity markets higher.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative when both series grow concurrently over a 10-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive during industrial production contractions, or the signal loses statistical significance when filtered for manufacturing downturn regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and improving profit margins, which systematically lifts equity valuations and generates positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal yields negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 257.0, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive when CPI crosses the threshold, or the inflation signal fails to predict direction in over 50% of historical threshold crossings.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with declining industrial production signals a productivity drag and wage-price spiral risk, prompting risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns are positive when payrolls rise alongside falling industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals tightening mortgage credit and weakening consumer leverage, which reduces downstream demand and triggers equity market declines.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following housing starts drops below 1.1 million, or the signal fails to maintain a negative directionality in credit contraction regimes over a 3-year test window.
